notes.anchor: divided up the river-bank into lots of so-many iugera apiece
notes.note: Damasippus, a speculative property dealer, had parcelled the Tiber bank (trans Tiberim) into standard-sized building lots at fixed prices. A iugerum is a Roman unit of land, about a quarter of a modern acre.

notes.anchor: Our Attica's illness disturbs me deeply
notes.note: Attica was Atticus's young daughter, whose recurring illness runs as an anxious thread through the early letters of book 12. Cicero half-suspects neglect but credits the household's care.
